new_classes.label_en: Wine with PGI in containers ≤ 2 L with actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol (CN 2204 22 79)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.g.A. in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L mit tatsächlichem Alkoholgehalt ≤ 15% vol (KN 2204 22 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, with an actual alcoholic strength not exceeding 15% vol, as specified in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 of the CN.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) PGI quality (via PGIOriginQuality restriction), (2) container volume ≤ 2 L (via SmallContainer restriction), and (3) actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol (via decimalRange on alcoholByVolumePercent). The legal text references additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, which defines the precise conditions for PGI wines (e.g. specific labelling or certification requirements for PGI status). The content of additional note 6(a) is not reproduced in the provided legal notes, so any sub-criteria it contains (e.g. minimum total dry extract, sugar content bands, or specific production area conditions) cannot be axiomatised here. The sibling code 22042280 covers PGI wine in the same container size but presumably with alcoholic strength > 15% vol — this disjoint boundary is partially implied by the decimalRange restriction. Coverage is therefore estimated at 0.75 due to the unresolved additional note 6(a) content.
